description: Phase 1: Path Security â€” ValidatedPath type with traversal, URL-encoded, and absolute path rejection
steps: Implement `ValidatedPath::new(path)` constructor that validates: non-empty, no `..` components, no `%2e%2e` URL-encoded traversal, no absolute paths (Unix `/`, Windows `C:\`, UNC `\\`), no null bytes
Implement `ValidatedPath::folder_name()` that returns the final path component
Add test: `validate_empty_path` â€” empty string rejected
Add test: `validate_valid_paths` â€” parameterized test for simple, nested, deeply nested, dashes, underscores, dots
Add test: `plugin_path_new_valid` â€” constructs validated path successfully
Add test: `plugin_path_new_invalid_traversal` â€” traversal rejected at construction
Add test: `plugin_path_display` â€” Display trait roundtrip
Add test: `plugin_path_folder_name` â€” extracts final component from multi-segment path
Add test: `plugin_path_folder_name_simple` â€” extracts name from single-segment path

description: Phase 1: LockedFile â€” OS-level exclusive file locking utility for cache and registry
steps: Implement `LockedFile::open(path)` â€” create parent dirs, open or create file (read+write, no truncate), acquire blocking exclusive lock via `fs2::FileExt::lock_exclusive()`
Implement `LockedFile::read_content(&mut self) -> Result<String>` â€” seek to start, read all content
Implement `LockedFile::write_content(&mut self, content: &str) -> Result<()>` â€” truncate to 0, seek to start, write all, flush
Lock is released on drop via `fs2::FileExt::unlock()` or file close
Add test: `read_write_roundtrip` â€” write content, read back, verify match
Add test: `write_truncates_previous_content` â€” write long, write short, verify only short remains
Add test: `lock_released_on_drop` â€” second open succeeds after first dropped

description: Phase 1: Platform Checker â€” runtime OS detection and compatibility checking
steps: Create `crates/libaipm/src/platform/mod.rs` with `Platform` enum (Windows, Linux, MacOs, Unknown(String))
Implement `Platform::from_str()` and `Display` with lowercase canonical strings
Implement custom `Deserialize` that preserves unknown values as `Unknown(String)`
Add test: `compatibility_universal_when_empty_platforms` â€” empty vec = universal

description: Phase 2: Download Cache â€” TTL-based cache with 5 policies, GC, and per-entry TTL
steps: Create `crates/libaipm/src/cache/mod.rs` with `DownloadCache` struct (root, policy, ttl, gc_days)
Define `CachePolicy` enum: Auto, CacheOnly, SkipCache, ForceRefresh, CacheNoRefresh
Implement `CachePolicy` serde (serialize to string, deserialize case-insensitive), `Display`, `FromStr`
Define `CacheEntry` struct: spec, dir_name (UUID), fetched_at, last_accessed, installed (bool), ttl_secs (Option)
Implement `CacheEntry::is_stale(global_ttl)` â€” checks per-entry TTL override then global TTL
Implement `get(spec_key) -> Result<Option<PathBuf>>` â€” skip for SkipCache/ForceRefresh; check index; validate directory exists; check staleness for Auto; touch last_accessed; return path
Implement `put(spec_key, source_dir, ttl_secs) -> Result<PathBuf>` â€” fail for SkipCache; copy to UUID dir; update index under lock; remove old dir
Implement `gc() -> Result<()>` â€” remove stale entries (last_accessed > gc_days, not installed); remove unreferenced dirs older than gc_days; preserve young dirs
Implement `with_policy(policy) -> Self` â€” clone with shared root
Add test: `cache_policy_roundtrip` â€” serialize/parse all 5 variants
Add test: `cache_put_and_get` â€” store and retrieve with content verification
Add test: `cache_stale_entry_returns_none` â€” TTL=0 immediate staleness

description: Phase 3: Plugin Spec Parser â€” unified parsing for source:identifier and name@version formats
steps: Create `crates/libaipm/src/spec/mod.rs` with `PluginSpec` enum: Registry, Local, Git, Marketplace
Define `GitPluginSource` struct: url, owner, repo, path (ValidatedPath), git_ref (Option<String>)
Define `MarketLocation` enum: GitHub { owner, repo }, Git { url }, Local { path }
Define `PluginSpecError` enum with variants for each failure mode
Implement `TryFrom<&str>` for `PluginSpec`: detect colon prefix for source type, fall back to name@version
Implement `PluginSpec::canonical_key()` â€” strips @ref suffix for identity comparison
Implement `PluginSpec::git_ref()` â€” extracts ref from spec
Implement `PluginSpec::folder_name()` â€” source-specific folder name derivation
Implement `PluginSpec::source_name()` â€” returns source type string for telemetry
Implement `PluginSpec::canonicalize()` â€” resolves local paths to absolute
Add test: `canonicalize_local_existing` â€” path resolution works
Add test: `canonicalize_local_nonexistent` â€” error for missing path
Add test: `parse_plugin_spec_invalid_format` â€” no colon and not a valid name = error
Add test: `parse_plugin_spec_unknown_source` â€” unknown source type = error
Add test: `parse_plugin_spec_empty_identifier` â€” empty after colon = error
Add test: `plugin_spec_display_local/git/github/marketplace` â€” Display trait for each
Add test: `plugin_spec_serde_roundtrip` â€” serialize then deserialize

description: Phase 4: Plugin Acquirer â€” local copy, git clone, and source redirect
steps: Define `AcquireError` variants: GitClone, PathNotFound, EmptyPluginDirectory, TooManyFiles, LocalNotFound, LocalNotDirectory, CopyFailed, Validation, SourceRedirectLoop
Implement `acquire_local(path, dest_dir) -> Result<PathBuf>` â€” verify exists, is directory, copy via fs_extra, validate structure

Implement `acquire_git(source, dest_dir) -> Result<PathBuf>` â€” check allowlist, create temp dir, run `git clone --depth=1 [--branch ref] url temp`, copy subdirectory if path specified, delete temp, validate structure
Implement `acquire_plugin(spec, dest_dir, engine) -> Result<PathBuf>` â€” dispatch to local/git/marketplace based on spec variant
Implement source redirect: after acquisition, check `[package.source]` in acquired `aipm.toml`; if present, parse redirect, delete stub, re-acquire (max 1 redirect)
Implement file count safety check: reject plugins with >500 files
Add test: `acquire_local_valid_plugin` â€” copy and verify content
Add test: `acquire_local_not_found` â€” error for missing directory
Add test: `acquire_local_not_directory` â€” error for file path
Add test: `source_redirect_max_one_level` â€” no recursive redirects

description: Phase 5: Engine Validator â€” two-tier validation (aipm.toml then marker files) with engines field
steps: Implement `PluginEngine::marker_paths()` returning engine-specific marker file paths
Implement `PluginEngine::marketplace_manifest_path()` returning engine-specific marketplace manifest path
Implement `validate_plugin(plugin_dir, engine) -> Result<()>`: 1) check for aipm.toml, if found read `engines` field and compare; 2) if no aipm.toml, fall back to engine-specific marker files
Add `engines` field to `Package` type in `crates/libaipm/src/manifest/types.rs` as `Option<Vec<String>>`

description: Phase 6: Global Installed Registry â€” persistent JSON registry with engine scoping
steps: Implement `install(spec, engines, cache_policy, cache_ttl_secs)` â€” new plugin or additive merge; name conflict detection (case-insensitive folder name across overlapping engines)
Implement `validate_install(spec, engines)` â€” pre-flight name conflict check
Implement `uninstall_engine(spec, engines_to_remove)` â€” expand 'all' if needed, remove engines, full uninstall if none remain
Implement `resolve_spec(identifier, engine_filter)` â€” exact spec or folder-name shorthand with engine disambiguation
Implement `engines_overlap(a, b)` â€” case-insensitive, empty = all
Add test: `engines_overlap` variants (both empty, one empty, matching, no match, case insensitive)

description: Phase 7: Marketplace Client â€” TOML manifest parsing, spec parsing, marketplace acquisition
steps: Create `crates/libaipm/src/marketplace/mod.rs` with top-level types and re-exports
Create `crates/libaipm/src/marketplace/manifest.rs` â€” TOML marketplace manifest parsing
Define `PluginSource` enum: RelativePath(String), Git { url, path, ref, sha }, Unsupported { source_type }
Implement `parse_marketplace_spec(spec)` â€” split on `@`, validate name, parse `#ref`, parse location (GitHub short, full URL, local path)
Implement `parse_market_location(location)` â€” detect URL vs local path vs GitHub short format
Implement `MarketplaceManifest::plugin_root()` â€” get non-empty plugin root
Implement marketplace acquisition in acquirer: download marketplace manifest, find plugin, resolve source, acquire from source
Add test: `parse_invalid_github_format` â€” single segment error
Add test: `parse_unknown_url_type` â€” gitlab etc. error
Add test: `parse_special_chars_in_plugin_name` â€” underscores, dots, uppercase
Add test: `parse_multiple_at_symbols` â€” URL with username
Add test: `parse_github_special_chars_in_location` â€” hyphens, underscores, dots
Add test: `parse_local_path_hash_not_treated_as_ref` â€” `#` in local path is literal
Add test: `parse_manifest` â€” basic TOML with 2 plugins

description: Phase 8: Source Allowlist â€” configurable trusted source patterns with CI enforcement
steps: Define config structure in `~/.aipm/config.toml`: `[security] allowed_sources = [...], enforce_allowlist = false`
Implement `is_source_allowed(url, config)` â€” glob-style pattern matching with `*` wildcards, case-insensitive
Implement enforcement check: if `enforce_allowlist` or `AIPM_ENFORCE_ALLOWLIST=1` env var â†’ reject non-matching; otherwise â†’ warn via tracing
Registry sources always allowed (trust the registry)

description: Phase 9: Manifest Integration â€” new dependency formats in aipm.toml for git/local/marketplace sources
steps: Extend `DependencySpec` in `crates/libaipm/src/manifest/types.rs` to support git, github, local path, and marketplace source objects
Add `git`, `github`, `path`, `marketplace` fields to `DetailedDependency` struct (all optional, mutually exclusive with `version`)
Add `ref` and `path` (subdirectory) fields to detailed git/github dependencies
Add `name` and `ref` fields to detailed marketplace dependencies
Update `crates/libaipm/src/manifest/validate.rs` to validate new dependency formats
Update manifest parsing to handle new TOML structures: `dep = { git = "url", path = "subdir", ref = "main" }`
Update `build_lockfile` in `crates/libaipm/src/installer/pipeline.rs` to produce git source lockfile entries
Update `build_resolution_from_lockfile` to parse git source lockfile entries
Integrate acquirer into install pipeline: detect source deps, acquire via cache, then store/link
Add engine validation and platform checking to install pipeline post-acquisition

description: Phase 9: CLI Integration â€” add --global, --engine, and source spec support to aipm install/uninstall/list
steps: Add `--global` flag to `aipm install` command in `crates/aipm/src/` CLI definition
Add `--engine <name>` flag to `aipm install` and `aipm uninstall` commands
Add `--plugin-cache <policy>` flag to `aipm install` for cache policy override
Update install handler: if `--global`, route to InstalledRegistry instead of project manifest
Update uninstall handler: support `--global` and `--engine` flags
Add `aipm list --global` to list globally installed plugins
Parse plugin spec argument as `PluginSpec` (supports both `name@version` and `source:identifier`)

description: E2E: Install plugin from local path and verify it is linked correctly
steps: Scenario: install from local path via CLI spec `local:./fixtures/my-plugin`
And a local plugin directory at `./fixtures/my-plugin` with `aipm.toml`
Then the plugin is copied to `.aipm/plugins/my-plugin/`
And the lockfile contains a `path+` source entry for `my-plugin`
And the plugin directory is linked in the expected output location
Scenario: install from local path via manifest `my-plugin = { path = "./fixtures/my-plugin" }`
Given an `aipm.toml` with a local path dependency
Then the plugin is installed from the local path
Scenario: local path not found produces clear error
Then the exit code is non-zero
And the error message mentions the path does not exist

description: E2E: Install plugin from git repository and verify cache behavior
steps: Scenario: install from git URL via CLI spec
And a git repository containing a plugin at a subdirectory
Then the plugin is cloned, subdirectory extracted, and linked
And the cache directory `~/.aipm/cache/entries/` contains a cached copy
Scenario: second install uses cache (Auto policy)
When I run `aipm install git:<repo-url>:path/to/plugin@main` again
Then the plugin is served from cache (no git clone)
Scenario: install with --plugin-cache skip bypasses cache
When I run `aipm install --plugin-cache skip git:<repo-url>:path/to/plugin@main`
Then a fresh git clone is performed
Then it expands to git clone of github.com/owner/repo
Scenario: git clone failure produces clear error
Then the exit code is non-zero and error mentions clone failure

description: E2E: Marketplace plugin acquisition from TOML manifest
steps: Scenario: install from marketplace via CLI
Given a marketplace repository with a `marketplace.toml` listing available plugins
Then the marketplace manifest is fetched and parsed
And the `hello-skills` plugin is acquired from its declared source
And the plugin is linked in the project
Scenario: marketplace plugin not found produces error
Then the error message lists available plugins from the marketplace
Scenario: marketplace with git source object
Given a marketplace entry with `[plugins.source] type = "git"`
When I install the plugin via marketplace
Then the plugin is fetched from the external git repository
Scenario: marketplace with relative path source
Given a marketplace entry with `source = "plugins/my-plugin"` (relative path)
Then the plugin is extracted from within the marketplace repo
Then relative sources are resolved under the plugin root

description: E2E: Global plugin install/uninstall with engine scoping
steps: Scenario: install plugin globally for all engines
Then `~/.aipm/installed.json` contains an entry with empty engines (all)
Scenario: install plugin globally for specific engine
When I run `aipm install --global --engine claude local:./fixtures/my-plugin`
Scenario: add second engine to existing global plugin
When I run `aipm install --global --engine copilot local:./fixtures/my-plugin`
Then the entry is removed from `installed.json`
Scenario: uninstall specific engine from global plugin
Given a plugin installed for claude and copilot
When I run `aipm uninstall --global --engine claude local:./fixtures/my-plugin`
Scenario: uninstall last engine removes plugin entirely
Given a plugin installed for claude only
When I run `aipm uninstall --global --engine claude local:./fixtures/my-plugin`
Then the plugin is fully removed
Then all globally installed plugins are listed with their engines
Scenario: name conflict between global plugins on overlapping engines
Given `my-plugin` installed globally from github for all engines
When I run `aipm install --global local:./my-plugin` (same folder name, different source)
Then the install fails with a name conflict error

description: E2E: Download cache policies end-to-end
steps: Scenario: Auto policy caches on first install, serves from cache on second
Then the second install uses the cached copy
Scenario: CacheOnly policy fails when not cached
When I run `aipm install --plugin-cache cache-only git:url`
Then the install fails with cache miss error
Scenario: SkipCache policy never reads or writes cache
Then no cache entry is created
Scenario: ForceRefresh always re-downloads and updates cache
Then a fresh download occurs and the cache entry is replaced
Scenario: CacheNoRefresh uses stale cache without re-downloading
Given a cached plugin past its TTL
Then the stale cached copy is used without downloading

description: E2E: Engine validation post-install
steps: Scenario: plugin with matching engine in aipm.toml installs successfully
Given a plugin with `engines = ["claude"]` in aipm.toml
When I install it targeting Claude engine
Scenario: plugin with non-matching engine produces warning
Given a plugin with `engines = ["copilot"]` in aipm.toml
When I install it targeting Claude engine
Then a warning is emitted about engine incompatibility
Scenario: plugin without aipm.toml falls back to marker file validation
Given a plugin with `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` but no aipm.toml
When I install it targeting Claude engine
Then the install succeeds via marker file fallback
Scenario: plugin without aipm.toml and no markers fails validation
Given a plugin with neither aipm.toml nor marker files
Then the install fails with a descriptive validation error

description: E2E: Platform compatibility checking at install time
steps: Scenario: plugin with no platform restrictions installs on any OS
Then the install succeeds with no warning
Scenario: plugin compatible with current platform installs successfully
Given a plugin with the current OS in `environment.platforms`
Scenario: plugin incompatible with current platform produces warning
Given a plugin declaring only platforms that don't match current OS
Then a warning is emitted listing declared vs current platforms

description: E2E: Source redirect follows stub to external repo
steps: Scenario: plugin with source redirect is re-acquired from redirect target
Given a marketplace plugin that is a stub with `[package.source]` pointing to an external repo
Then the stub is deleted and the real plugin is fetched from the redirect URL
And the final plugin directory contains the real code, not the stub
Given a plugin whose redirect target also has `[package.source]`
Then the install fails with a redirect loop error

description: E2E: Source allowlist enforcement in CI mode
steps: Scenario: disallowed source warns when not enforced
When I install from a non-allowed source
Then the install succeeds with a warning
Scenario: disallowed source fails when enforced
When I install from a non-allowed source
Then the install fails with a security error
Scenario: local sources always pass allowlist
When I install `local:./my-plugin` with enforcement on
Then the install succeeds regardless of allowlist contents

description: E2E: Path security â€” traversal attempts in plugin specs are rejected
steps: Scenario: path traversal in git spec is rejected
Then the install fails with a path traversal error
Scenario: URL-encoded traversal in spec is rejected
Then the install fails with a path traversal error
Scenario: absolute path in git spec is rejected
Then the install fails with an absolute path error

description: E2E: Mixed registry and source dependencies in a single project
steps: Scenario: project with both registry and git source dependencies
Given an `aipm.toml` with `dep-a = "^1.0"` and `dep-b = { git = "url", ref = "main" }`
Then dep-a is resolved from the registry
And dep-b is acquired via git clone
And both are linked in the plugins directory
And the lockfile has correct source types for each
And git source deps are re-fetched (ForceRefresh policy)

description: Developer documentation: Installing plugins from local paths
steps: Document the `local:` spec format: `aipm install local:./path/to/plugin`
Include example project layout showing local plugin directory structure
Document validation requirements (aipm.toml or engine marker files)
Document behavior: plugin is copied (not symlinked) to project plugins directory
Document lockfile entry format for local sources
Add troubleshooting section for common errors (path not found, not a directory)

description: Developer documentation: Installing plugins from git repositories
steps: Document the `aipm.toml` format: `my-plugin = { git = "url", path = "subdir", ref = "main" }` and `{ github = "org/repo", path = "subdir", ref = "v2.0" }`
Explain authentication: uses system git credential helper (git-credential-manager, etc.)
Document how to configure credentials for private repos
Document shallow clone behavior (--depth=1, subdirectory extraction)
Document cache behavior: Auto policy caches for 24 hours by default
Document `--plugin-cache` flag for overriding cache policy
Add examples for common scenarios: public GitHub plugin, private GitHub plugin, self-hosted git

description: Developer documentation: Installing plugins from marketplaces
steps: Document how to configure marketplaces in `~/.aipm/config.toml`: `[marketplaces] name = "location"`
Document the marketplace manifest TOML format with examples
Document source types in marketplace manifests: relative paths, git objects, unsupported types
Add examples: browsing marketplace plugins, installing, pinning to a ref

description: Developer documentation: Global plugin installation and engine scoping
steps: Document `aipm list --global` to view global plugins
Explain engine scoping: empty = all engines, specific = restricted
Explain additive engine behavior on re-install
Document per-plugin cache policy and TTL configuration
Document name conflict rules and how to resolve them
Add examples: install for all, install for Claude only, uninstall one engine, resolve ambiguous spec

description: Developer documentation: Cache management and policies
steps: Document the 5 cache policies: Auto, CacheOnly, SkipCache, ForceRefresh, CacheNoRefresh
Document default TTL (24 hours) and GC threshold (30 days)
Document GC behavior: stale entries removed, installed entries preserved
Document offline/CI workflows: CacheOnly for air-gapped environments, CacheNoRefresh for reproducible builds
Add examples for each cache policy scenario

description: Developer documentation: Source security and allowlists
steps: Document CI/CD setup: set env var in pipeline to enforce trust
Document that local and registry sources are always trusted
Document path traversal protection (automatic, no configuration needed)
Add examples: configuring for a GitHub organization, private git host, CI pipeline

description: Developer documentation: Engine compatibility and platform requirements
steps: Document two-tier validation: aipm.toml first, engine marker files fallback
Document engine marker file paths for Claude and Copilot
Document platform detection and compatibility checking at install time
Document forward compatibility: unknown engine/platform values preserved
Add examples: plugin for Claude only, universal plugin, platform-restricted plugin

description: Coverage gate: achieve 89% branch coverage across all new modules
steps: Run `cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --workspace --branch` to collect test coverage
Run `cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --doc` to collect doctest coverage
Run `cargo +nightly llvm-cov report --doctests --branch --ignore-filename-regex '(tests/|research/|specs/|wizard_tty\.rs)'` to check branch coverage >= 89%
Identify any new modules below 89% branch coverage
Add targeted tests to reach the threshold for each module
Verify `cargo build --workspace`, `cargo test --workspace`, `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check` all pass with zero warnings
